alkylation reagent: we used iodoacetamide (IAA)/4-acetamido-4′-maleimidylstilbene-2,2′-disulfonic acid (AMS)-based reverse thiol trapping approach
alkylation concentration: alkylation using 1 μl of 500 mM methyl methanethiosulfonate
cleavage agent: 4 μg of sequencing-grade trypsin was added to the mixture
instrument: the pooled, plexed samples were then dried under vacuum, resolubilized in 1% trifluoroacetic acid, and finally desalted using 2 μg capacity ZipTips (Millipore) according to manufacturer instructions. Peptides were then eluted into a Fusion Tribrid Mass Spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific)
ionization type: Ions were created at 1.7 kV using an EASY Spray source
labeling: Tandem mass tags (TMT), coupled with liquid chromatography–tandem mass spectrometry (LC–MS/MS) proteomic analysis
reduction reagent: reduced with 1 μl of 120 mm TCEP [Tris (2-carboxyethyl) phosphine hydrochloride]
reduction concentration: reduced with 1 μl of 120 mm TCEP
